title: [Feature]: Add ordered-consumer client compatibility and fail-closed capability checks
problem: NATS ordered-consumer support varies by client API generation. The current nats-py runtime used by nats-sinks exposes ordinary subscribe and pull-subscribe helpers but does not expose an obvious high-level ordered-consumer helper through the imported JetStreamContext API. A future inspection feature needs compatibility detection before it can be production-quality.
proposal: Add a small compatibility layer that detects whether the installed NATS Python client supports ordered consumers through a documented public API. If support is absent or ambiguous, ordered inspection features must fail closed with a clear message. The layer should isolate client-version checks from delivery logic and should avoid dynamic imports or private API use unless a future design review explicitly approves and tests it.
users: Maintainers implementing ordered inspection tooling, operators pinning dependency versions, and security reviewers who need unsupported client behavior to fail safely.
delivery_semantics: Compatibility checks must not affect the production durable pull runner. They should only gate explicit inspection or replay tooling. Unsupported ordered-consumer support must prevent the inspection command from running rather than falling back to a different delivery mode silently.
security: Capability errors must not print credentials, URLs, subject values, or environment details. Dependency-version reporting should be minimal and suitable for public issue evidence.
acceptance: Compatibility detection uses documented public client APIs where available.
Unsupported or ambiguous client support fails closed.
Tests cover supported, unsupported, and ambiguous capability states.
Documentation explains dependency and client-version limitations.
tests: Add unit tests with fake client objects for supported, unsupported, partial, and exception-raising capability states. Run scripts/check.sh.
documentation: Update ordered-consumer evaluation, dependency management, CLI, test-report, and changelog documentation.
closeout: Close after the release containing ordered-consumer compatibility checks is published. Include release tag, tests, documentation, and limitations.
